page: Amazon Web Services m6a.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AMD EPYC 7R13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Network performance is anchored by a 25 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and PassMark CPU evaluations, particularly in encryption and extended instructions. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks is highly efficient, though uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the average range. Cryptographic and integer operations are accelerated by hardware flags such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. Additionally, the system demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations, software compilation, and small-model LLM inference, while text processing and static web serving throughput remain average.

From a resource density perspective, the m6a.16xlarge offers a balanced hardware profile suitable for workloads requiring substantial memory and compute without the premium of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The lack of bundled local NVMe storage means all disk I/O is dependent on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy operations despite the 25 Gbps network baseline.

This server configuration is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, software compilation pipelines, and medium-scale web serving. It is also appropriate for containerized microservices, Redis caching layers, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 RAM but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. The instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, high cached memory bandwidth, and excellent cryptographic execution, supported by a 25 Gbps baseline network. While uncached memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput are average, its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers a cost-efficient profile for resource-dense workloads. It is highly suited for database operations, software compilation, Redis caching, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.16xlarge is a general-purpose server with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 server configuration delivering sixty-four virtual cores and two hundred fifty-six gigabytes of memory for diverse enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
Equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 RAM and a 25 Gbps baseline network
Strong multi-core CPU, cryptographic, and database benchmark performance
No local storage or dedicated GPU accelerators included
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and Redis caching
